_comment: AgenticOS vertical-resize tier ladder — source of truth for the human-gated autoscale workflow (.github/workflows/agenticos-autoscale.yml, GOL-55).
_lineage: Read by the resize job at runtime. The workflow resolves a named tier -> DO size slug, guards the target against the allowlist + live droplet, then calls `doctl compute droplet-action resize --resize-disk=false --wait`. Changing a slug here is the ONLY supported way to change what the workflow can resize to.
_governance: Human-gated only. `workflow_dispatch` (a person clicking Run, incl. the one-click link in Discord capacity alerts) is the sole apply path by default. The `schedule` trigger is notify-only unless repo variable AUTOSCALE_SCHEDULED_APPLY=true is set AND the `agenticos-resize` GitHub Environment has a required reviewer. No autonomous prod apply. See docs/ADR/009-human-gated-vertical-autoscale.md.

droplet.name: agenticos-droplet
droplet._note: Hard-pinned. The workflow refuses to act on any other droplet id as a blast-radius guard.
droplet._verified_baseline: Confirmed live 2026-07-04 via DO API (token from GOL-59): size=s-2vcpu-4gb, mem=4096MB, vcpus=2, disk=80GB, $24/mo, region=nyc1, status=active. The box is already at the 'base' tier (post-P0 right-size resting state).

reversibility._rule: With --resize-disk=false the disk never changes. A resize is therefore only allowed to a target plan whose nominal disk_gb is >= the droplet's CURRENT actual disk. The workflow fetches the live disk at runtime and refuses any target that would require shrinking the disk (DO would reject it anyway; we fail fast with a clear message). The safe reversible operating band is the set of tiers that share the current disk footprint.
reversibility._founder_band: The intended day-to-day band is base <-> busy (scale up for market days / launches, back down when quiet). Both keep the current disk via --resize-disk=false, so the round-trip is fully reversible.

quiet._use: Off-peak floor — CURRENTLY UNREACHABLE. The live droplet has an 80GB disk (confirmed 2026-07-04); a --resize-disk=false resize cannot shrink 80GB -> 60GB, so the workflow will refuse this target. Retained for reference only; it becomes usable only if the droplet is ever rebuilt at <=60GB disk. The real day-to-day floor is 'base'.

base._use: Default steady-state after the P0 one-tier right-size. Recommended resting tier.

busy._use: Market days, launches, batch agent runs. Doubles vCPU + RAM. Keeps the current (smaller) disk via --resize-disk=false, so it round-trips back to base cleanly.

defaults._note: Discord capacity alerts and the scheduled reminders link to workflow_dispatch pre-filled with these tiers.

_verified: Ladder validated against the live DO API 2026-07-04 (token from GOL-59): all three slugs exist + are available in nyc1 with exactly these disk/mem/price values (s-2vcpu-2gb=60GB/$18, s-2vcpu-4gb=80GB/$24, s-4vcpu-8gb=160GB/$48). Live droplet is s-2vcpu-4gb/80GB = 'base'. Practical reversible band = base<->busy (both keep the 80GB disk). 'quiet' is retained but unreachable from the current 80GB disk (kept in the allowlist so the runtime guard produces a clear disk-shrink refusal rather than a mysterious slug error). No ladder correction was needed — the placeholder values were already correct.
